examples.title: Carnot Efficiency Calculator
examples.description: Algorithm to calculate Carnot efficiency given hot and cold reservoir temperatures
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CalculateCarnotEfficiency
INPUT: Th (hot temp in Kelvin), Tc (cold temp in Kelvin)
OUTPUT: Efficiency as percentage

BEGIN
 // Validate inputs (temperatures must be positive)
 IF Th <= 0 OR Tc <= 0 THEN
 PRINT "Error: Temperatures must be in Kelvin and positive"
 RETURN -1
 END IF
 
 // Check if Th > Tc (second law requirement)
 IF Tc >= Th THEN
 PRINT "Error: Hot temp must be greater than cold temp"
 RETURN -1
 END IF
 
 // Calculate Carnot efficiency
 efficiency = 1 - (Tc / Th)
 
 // Convert to percentage
 efficiency_percentage = efficiency * 100
 
 PRINT "Carnot Efficiency: " + efficiency_percentage + "%"
 RETURN efficiency_percentage
END
examples.explanation: The algorithm calculates theoretical maximum efficiency using Carnot formula: η = 1 - Tc/Th. This represents the upper limit for any heat engine operating between two temperatures.

examples.title: Thermal Power Plant Efficiency Calculation
examples.description: Calculate actual efficiency of thermal power plant given input and output parameters
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CalculatePlantEfficiency
INPUT: 
 fuel_consumed (kg)
 calorific_value (kJ/kg)
 electrical_output (kWh)
OUTPUT: Overall efficiency as percentage

BEGIN
 // Convert electrical output to kJ
 // 1 kWh = 3600 kJ
 energy_output_kj = electrical_output * 3600
 
 // Calculate energy input from fuel
 energy_input_kj = fuel_consumed * calorific_value
 
 // Calculate efficiency
 efficiency = (energy_output_kj / energy_input_kj) * 100
 
 PRINT "Plant Efficiency: " + efficiency + "%"
 PRINT "Energy Input: " + energy_input_kj + " kJ"
 PRINT "Energy Output: " + energy_output_kj + " kJ"
 
 // Compare with Carnot limit
 IF efficiency > 40 THEN
 PRINT "Warning: Efficiency exceeds typical thermal plant limits"
 END IF
 
 RETURN efficiency
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm computes actual power plant efficiency by comparing electrical energy output with chemical energy input from fuel. Typical efficiency is 30-40%.

examples.title: Calorific Value Comparison
examples.description: Compare calorific values of different fossil fuels
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CompareFossilFuels
INPUT: None (uses predefined values)
OUTPUT: Ranked list of fuels by calorific value

BEGIN
 // Define calorific values in kJ/kg
 fuel_data = [
 {"name": "Anthracite coal", "cv": 33000},
 {"name": "Bituminous coal", "cv": 28000},
 {"name": "Lignite", "cv": 17000},
 {"name": "Petroleum", "cv": 42000},
 {"name": "Natural gas", "cv": 48000},
 {"name": "LPG", "cv": 50000}
 ]
 
 // Sort by calorific value (descending)
 SORT fuel_data BY cv DESCENDING
 
 PRINT "Fossil Fuels Ranked by Calorific Value (kJ/kg):"
 FOR i FROM 0 TO length(fuel_data) - 1 DO
 PRINT (i+1) + ". " + fuel_data[i].name + ": " + fuel_data[i].cv
 END FOR
 
 // Calculate efficiency for same energy output
 target_energy = 1000000 // 1 GJ
 PRINT "\nFuel required for 1 GJ energy:"
 FOR each fuel IN fuel_data DO
 required_kg = target_energy / fuel.cv
 PRINT fuel.name + ": " + required_kg + " kg"
 END FOR
END
examples.explanation: This code ranks fossil fuels by their calorific values and calculates fuel requirements for a fixed energy output. Natural gas/LPG have highest values, requiring less fuel for same energy.
